verses.text: Moses begins, 'Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations,' for before the mountains were born, 'from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.' God is the eternal home of His people. The unchanging God is the refuge of every passing age.

Moses begins, 'Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations,' for before the mountains were born, 'from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.' God is the eternal home of His people. The unchanging God is the refuge of every passing age.

He turns man back to dust; a thousand years to Him are 'as yesterday,' and human life is like grass that flourishes at dawn and withers by evening. Our days are fleeting. Against God's eternity, the longest life is a brief, fading green.

He turns man back to dust; a thousand years to Him are 'as yesterday,' and human life is like grass that flourishes at dawn and withers by evening. Our days are fleeting. Against God's eternity, the longest life is a brief, fading green.

He turns man back to dust; a thousand years to Him are 'as yesterday,' and human life is like grass that flourishes at dawn and withers by evening. Our days are fleeting. Against God's eternity, the longest life is a brief, fading green.

He turns man back to dust; a thousand years to Him are 'as yesterday,' and human life is like grass that flourishes at dawn and withers by evening. Our days are fleeting. Against God's eternity, the longest life is a brief, fading green.

We are consumed by God's anger, our secret sins set in His light, our years passing 'as a tale that is told.' Sin shortens and saddens our days. The brevity of life is bound up with the judgment our sin deserves.

We are consumed by God's anger, our secret sins set in His light, our years passing 'as a tale that is told.' Sin shortens and saddens our days. The brevity of life is bound up with the judgment our sin deserves.

We are consumed by God's anger, our secret sins set in His light, our years passing 'as a tale that is told.' Sin shortens and saddens our days. The brevity of life is bound up with the judgment our sin deserves.

Our years are seventy, or eighty if strong, yet full of 'labour and sorrow,' soon cut off; who knows the power of God's anger? Life is short and hard. We seldom reckon how weighty God's displeasure truly is.

Our years are seventy, or eighty if strong, yet full of 'labour and sorrow,' soon cut off; who knows the power of God's anger? Life is short and hard. We seldom reckon how weighty God's displeasure truly is.
'So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.' He prays for a right view of time. To remember how few our days are is the beginning of a wise and holy life.

He asks God to return and have pity, to 'satisfy us early with thy mercy,' that joy may match the years of affliction. He longs for gladness from God. Only God's mercy can turn our short, sorrowful days into rejoicing.

He asks God to return and have pity, to 'satisfy us early with thy mercy,' that joy may match the years of affliction. He longs for gladness from God. Only God's mercy can turn our short, sorrowful days into rejoicing.

He asks God to return and have pity, to 'satisfy us early with thy mercy,' that joy may match the years of affliction. He longs for gladness from God. Only God's mercy can turn our short, sorrowful days into rejoicing.

Let God's work appear to His servants and 'the beauty of the LORD' rest on them, and 'establish thou the work of our hands.' He prays for lasting fruit. The brief labours of mortal men gain permanence only when God blesses them.
